flags.message: Outcome leakage in the provisioned baseline: the snapshot (record/snapshots/2026-07-13.json) itself contains this event's disposition — the May 11, 2026 docket entries record the petition's resolution and the ensuing judgment. I also recognize the case independently (companion to Allen v. Milligan). I predicted from the pre-decision record only (entries before the May 11 disposition, the BIO, statpack base rates, corpus priors) and made no retrieval about this case, but the cell should be treated as contaminated and discounted accordingly.

Stale cell state: record/context.json says mode=forward and event.yaml says resolved=false, but the case was decided on May 11, 2026 — two months before this run's snapshot date. The event should have been latched/resolved (outcome.json written) rather than fanned out as a live forward prediction; the ingestion/latch step appears not to have caught this disposition (possibly because the order is a GVR-style entry rather than a plain granted/denied line).
